summarization.a: The multi-spectra chart shows Fe 2p XAS (TEY) spectra of Fe₂O₃:MgO thin films grown at 425 °C. As Mg concentration increases from non-doped to 0.38 at.%, the Fe 2p peak intensities decrease and broaden, indicating changes in the local electronic structure with Mg incorporation.

data_extraction.a:
| Photon energy (eV) | Relative intensity |     Sample   |
| ------------------ | ------------------ | ------------ |
|      ~710          | High               | non-doped    |
|      ~724          | Medium             | non-doped    |
|      ~710          | High               | Mg 0.02 at.% |
|      ~724          | Medium             | Mg 0.02 at.% |
|      ~710          | Medium             | Mg 0.2 at.%  |
|      ~724          | Medium             | Mg 0.2 at.%  |
|      ~710          | Low                | Mg 0.38 at.% |
|      ~724          | Low                | Mg 0.38 at.% |

a.question: How does increasing Mg concentration affect the Fe 2p XAS spectra?
a.answer: Increasing Mg concentration leads to a reduction in Fe 2p peak intensity and broader spectral features, indicating modification of the Fe electronic environment due to Mg incorporation.

a.question: What measurement technique is used to obtain these spectra?
a.answer: X-ray Absorption Spectroscopy (XAS) in total electron yield (TEY) mode.

a.question: Are all spectra measured at the same growth temperature?
Which Mg concentrations are compared in the figure?
a.answer: Non-doped, 0.02 at.%, 0.2 at.%, and 0.38 at.% Mg.
